description: Single high-throughput service in Go (Gin/Echo/Chi) or Rust (Axum/Actix). Extracted from a modular monolith because (a) team owns it, (b) bounded context is provably-independent, (c) throughput / latency target requires it. NOT a default — earn your way in.

anti_recommendations.rewrite-from-monolith-without-bounded-co: kill — extract a service only when the second team needs to own it
anti_recommendations.rust-because-its-safer: warn — Rust learning curve is 6-12 months; do not pick without an on-team senior
anti_recommendations.go-without-context-everywhere: kill — context.Context on every handler + DB call mandatory
anti_recommendations.no-circuit-breakers: kill — extracted services need hystrix/gobreaker or equivalent
anti_recommendations.no-bulkhead-isolation: kill — connection pool isolation per dependency
anti_recommendations.shared-database-across-services: kill — defeats the point of the extraction

canon_references: Sam Newman, Building Microservices 2e (2021), ch. 3 'Splitting the Monolith'
Susan Fowler, Production-Ready Microservices (2017) — eight pillars
Niall Murphy + Betsy Beyer, SRE (2016) — circuit breakers + bulkheads
Tigran Bregadze, Production Rust at scale (talks, 2023-2024)
Pat Helland, Life beyond Distributed Transactions (2007)
